newsSinceJun6: Jun 8: stock spiked ~11% on reports Google placed a firm order for 3M+ TPUs on Intel 18A for 2028 delivery (potentially 6M units across 2027-28 per follow-on reports), and Nvidia is running early trials of Intel 18A/14A plus EMIB advanced packaging for its next-gen Feynman GPUs (Q3 decision window; ~25% of Feynman production reportedly in play). EMIB yields reported near 90% with Google TPU v8e (2H27) and Meta in-house CPU (2H28) as adopters; Cadence also expanded 14A PDK collaboration. Jun 9: -4.9% to $104.83 on profit-taking plus renewed focus on the ~$2.4B quarterly Intel Foundry operating loss and 2027 hyperscaler-capex worries - flow-driven giveback of Monday's news pop, thesis intact; consensus analyst PT (~$89) sits well below price.

sources.label: TrendForce - Intel Foundry gains momentum: Google reportedly orders 3M TPUs, Nvidia evaluates 18A (Jun 9, 2026)
Wccftech - Nvidia looks to Intel 18A/14A and EMIB packaging for Feynman AI chips
TrendForce - Intel EMIB gains traction at Google, Meta; yields said to reach ~90%
TradingKey - INTC down on Jun 9: foundry losses and 2027 datacenter-spend concerns
FXLeaders - Intel surges 11% on Google AI deal, foundry boom (Jun 9, 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: The Google 3M-TPU order on 18A directly answers this page's knock ('external foundry revenue still small') by converting option value into a booked anchor customer - meaningful new evidence since Jun 6. But Monday's +11% added roughly $50B of cap for 2028-dated revenue at currently negative foundry margins, more than pricing the news on an alpha lens; yesterday's down move already paid the crowding penalty and today's -4.9% is only a partial giveback, so hold at 97 with the score crediting the fundamental validation.
verdicts.bull: Google's 2028 TPU order (potentially 6M units across 2027-28) plus a live Nvidia Feynman 18A/EMIB evaluation makes Intel the only credible non-TSMC advanced-node-plus-packaging source in the West, with foundry revenue already growing 16% YoY.
verdicts.neutral: Order revenue lands in 2028 while IFS burns ~$2.4B per quarter; at $523B cap, +166% YTD, and ~18% above consensus PT, the booked wins are largely in the price and the residual is pure execution.
verdicts.bear: The reports are unconfirmed by either company; 18A yield or ramp slippage would push Google volumes out, Nvidia's evaluation may still resolve to TSMC, and a 2027 hyperscaler capex digestion hits the most rerated foundry story in the market hardest.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Official Intel/Google confirmation of the TPU agreement in guidance or backlog, and the Nvidia Feynman foundry/packaging decision expected in Q3 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: 18A yield/ramp execution failing to support 2028 Google volumes while foundry losses keep burning ~$3B/quarter of cash.

verdicts.rankRationale: Page-relevant news is the packaging leg: Nvidia evaluating EMIB for Feynman and ~90% EMIB yields with Google/Meta adoption validate the advanced-packaging half of this listing's thesis. But the silicon-photonics/CPO leg got no new evidence, the stock still trades entirely on the foundry/CPU turnaround as the thesis warns, and after the +11% news pop it is the most crowded name in semis - hold at 105 after yesterday's down move.
verdicts.bull: EMIB at ~90% yield with Google TPU v8e and Meta CPU adoption plus a possible Nvidia Feynman packaging slot makes Intel's packaging franchise a genuine second source to TSMC CoWoS - the under-appreciated leg of this listing.
verdicts.neutral: Packaging and photonics remain a small slice of a $523B-cap turnaround story; page-specific alpha is diluted by the dominant foundry/CPU narrative and the name is now widely owned after +166% YTD.
verdicts.bear: If the TPU order proves softer than reported or hyperscaler capex digests in 2027, the crowded momentum money exits fast and the packaging/photonics option value gets marked down with the foundry narrative.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Nvidia formally awarding EMIB packaging for Feynman in Q3, or a concrete silicon-photonics/CPO design-win disclosure tied to the TPU and Nvidia engagements.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding: a consensus AI-foundry trade where any 18A or packaging stumble produces outsized de-rating versus this page's hidden-alpha mandate.
